8.reason: [error: cannot initialize members in struct definition; use designated initializer when declaring a variable]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Cannot initialize struct members in C; this is C++ syntax. Use assignment in main() instead.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Cannot initialize struct members in C; this is C++ syntax. Use assignment in main() or leave uninitialized.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Invalid initialization inside struct definition in C (only C++ allows this)
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [error: cannot initialize members in struct definition; use designated initializer when declaring a variable]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  In C, struct members cannot be initialized directly within the struct definition. This is C++ syntax and causes a compilation error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  C does not allow initialization of struct members inside the struct definition
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [C does not allow initialization of struct members at declaration; this is a C++ feature]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [C does not allow initialization of struct members at declaration; this is a C++ feature]

33.reason: [Error: Assignment '=' used instead of equality '==' in condition; should be p[j].y0 == p[i].y1]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Assignment '=' used instead of equality '==' in condition; should be p[j].y0 == p[i].y1]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Use of assignment '=' instead of equality '==' in condition; should be p[j].y0 == p[i].y1]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Assignment '=' used instead of equality '=='; will always be true regardless of y values
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Logical error: uses assignment '=' instead of comparison '=='. This incorrectly assigns p[i].y1 to p[j].y0 and evaluates to true if the assigned value is non-zero.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Assignment operator '=' used instead of equality operator '=='; causes logical error and unintended assignment]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Assignment operator '=' used instead of equality operator '=='; causes logical error and unintended assignment]
